nodes.label: Playwright MCP Browser Automation
nodes.rationale: Each posting skill uses a fresh browser session per post via Playwright MCP to avoid cross-account contamination and ban risk; browser is always closed after each post regardless of success or failure.

nodes.label: Apps Script Web App (Sheet API Gateway)
nodes.rationale: Used as the HTTP gateway for reading and writing to Google Sheets without Python dependencies; provides instant HTTP GET/POST with no auth overhead for the agent.
nodes.norm_label: apps script web app (sheet api gateway)

nodes.label: UTM Parameter Tracking Convention
nodes.rationale: Every link embedded in posts or blogs carries UTM parameters (utm_source, utm_medium=social+organic or Referral, utm_campaign=Automation) to attribute traffic back to the originating platform and campaign.

nodes.label: Cloudinary Image Upload Pipeline
nodes.rationale: ChatGPT DALL-E generates the cover image via browser automation; the image is downloaded as base64, saved to a temp file, uploaded to Cloudinary via curl/SHA1 signature, and the returned secure_url is embedded in the blog HTML.

nodes.label: Three Distinct Blog Titles Rule
nodes.rationale: Every blog requires three separately-purposed titles (H1 for reader hook, Blog Title for CMS display, Blog SEO Title for meta/Google) that must be genuinely distinct in angle and length to serve their respective jobs without overlap.

nodes.label: Multi-Platform Batch Posting Schedule
nodes.rationale: X posts every batch (8/day), Facebook posts 5 batches/day, LinkedIn posts 3 batches/day to space platform activity and reduce ban risk; each batch slot has a defined IST time window.

nodes.label: SERP-Based Content Priority (P1/P2/P3)
nodes.rationale: URLs are ranked P1 (not indexed or trending), P2 (rank 31-60), or P3 (rank 1-30) based on Ken Research's position in Google search results; P1 content is prioritized for earliest posting slots to maximize SEO impact.

nodes.label: sheet_write.py Direct Sheets API Script
nodes.rationale: Used for all write operations to Google Sheets (X, FB, LI, Blog results) because it writes directly via the Sheets API without Apps Script overhead; large payloads like blog HTML are always written via a temp JSON file to avoid CLI length limits.
